reasoning: As of late 2024, Elon Musk holds a substantial lead of roughly $50–60 billion over the next richest individuals (Zuckerberg, Bezos, Arnault) on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index. While his wealth is heavily concentrated in Tesla, which can be volatile, his significant unlisted equity in SpaceX increasingly provides a stable offset. Predicting forward to December 31, 2025, Musk starts with an overwhelming structural advantage. Assuming standard market conditions, the #1 spot on such indices is sticky in periods without major macroeconomic crashes, and a lead of this magnitude would take an extreme downturn in Tesla shares combined with a huge surge for a competitor to overcome. Given that the knowledge cutoff is just two months before the resolution date (late October to December 2025), if he is still #1 by the cutoff, the stickiness over the remaining two months is extremely high (generally >90%). Accounting for the roughly 15–20% chance that he loses the lead entirely by October 2025 due to TSLA swings, the overall probability settles around 82%.
